source: Node migration/backout suites and Playwright standalone, keyboard, and reflow supplements

observed_signals: Deterministic read-only v2 import leaves the v1 input and Session Store snapshot unchanged.
Component, Point, element, feedback-thread, Choice, and Revision identities survive import.
Unsupported Workspace Kinds and orphaned feedback identities are rejected.
File import retains source bytes, refuses an existing destination, and rejects a symlink source.
CLI migration writes v2 side by side, preserves exact v1 bytes, and exports the original Session Store history.
CLI backout restores active version 1 while retaining the migrated v2 document byte for byte.
Publish refuses to overwrite the backed-out v1 document with v2 state.
A later migration reactivates the retained byte-identical v2 document without exposing its private path.
Backout rejects a non-v1 fallback and leaves version 2 active.

commands: npx playwright test --config skills/brainstorming/playwright.config.ts --project=e2e skills/brainstorming/e2e/workspace-host.spec.ts skills/brainstorming/e2e/architecture-canvas.spec.ts --grep keyboard frame navigation|reflows at 320 CSS pixels|keyboard and mobile usable
observed_signals: A network-disabled v2 standalone export preserves Product Choice state, evidence, feedback history, and read-only Revision state.
Direct v1 rendering preserves derived Point and element feedback targets.
Keyboard frame navigation, visible focus, and session-persisted density remain operable.
The shared host reflows at 320 CSS pixels and keeps controls reachable with reduced motion.
Architecture mode, focus, annotation, and camera controls remain keyboard and mobile usable.

residual_risks: Browser supplements were observed in headless Chromium; supported CI platforms still require complete repository validation.
Filesystem non-overwrite and symlink behavior was observed on the current local platform.
